prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_api_endpoint_specification server_impl_1)
(create_api_endpoint_specification consumer_impl_1)
(create_api_endpoint_specification api_contract_document_alpha)
(create_api_endpoint_specification api_contract_document_beta)
(assign_interface_template_to_endpoint server_impl_1 interface_template_rest)
(assign_interface_template_to_endpoint consumer_impl_1 interface_template_grpc)
(assign_interface_template_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha interface_template_websocket)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(validate_and_activate_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(validate_and_activate_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(validate_and_activate_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(assign_data_schema_to_implementation server_impl_1 message_channel_events data_schema_user)
(bind_schema_to_implementation_channel server_impl_1 message_channel_events operation_get_resource data_schema_user)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(assign_data_schema_to_consumer consumer_impl_1 deployment_target_staging data_schema_user)
(bind_schema_to_consumer_channel consumer_impl_1 deployment_target_staging operation_get_resource data_schema_user)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(assemble_release_artifact_for_deployment server_impl_1 consumer_impl_1 message_channel_events deployment_target_staging release_artifact_package_1)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(verify_release_artifact_against_implementation release_artifact_package_1 server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(mark_implementation_compliant_after_release server_impl_1 release_artifact_package_1)
(mark_consumer_compliant_after_release consumer_impl_1 release_artifact_package_1)
(attach_test_suite_to_artifact api_contract_document_alpha test_suite_unit release_artifact_package_1)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(apply_test_results_and_stage_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha test_suite_unit release_artifact_package_1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(attach_governance_policy_to_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha governance_policy_security)
(bind_authentication_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha auth_jwt_token)
(apply_governance_policy_review api_contract_document_alpha governance_policy_security auth_jwt_token test_suite_unit release_artifact_package_1)
(attach_extension_to_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha extension_module_metrics)
(finalize_extension_review api_contract_document_alpha extension_module_metrics test_suite_unit release_artifact_package_1)
(finalize_and_publish_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(assign_contract_version_to_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha contract_version_v1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha operation_get_resource)
(provision_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha interface_template_websocket contract_version_v1)
(assign_interface_template_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta interface_template_websocket)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(validate_and_activate_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(attach_test_suite_to_artifact api_contract_document_beta test_suite_integration release_artifact_package_1)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(apply_test_results_and_stage_contract_document api_contract_document_beta test_suite_integration release_artifact_package_1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(detach_governance_policy_from_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha governance_policy_security)
(attach_governance_policy_to_contract_document api_contract_document_beta governance_policy_security)
(unbind_authentication_from_endpoint api_contract_document_alpha auth_jwt_token)
(bind_authentication_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta auth_jwt_token)
(apply_governance_policy_review api_contract_document_beta governance_policy_security auth_jwt_token test_suite_integration release_artifact_package_1)
(detach_extension_from_contract_document api_contract_document_alpha extension_module_metrics)
(attach_extension_to_contract_document api_contract_document_beta extension_module_metrics)
(finalize_extension_review api_contract_document_beta extension_module_metrics test_suite_integration release_artifact_package_1)
(finalize_and_publish_contract_document api_contract_document_beta)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(assign_contract_version_to_endpoint api_contract_document_beta contract_version_v1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint api_contract_document_beta operation_get_resource)
(provision_contract_document api_contract_document_beta interface_template_websocket contract_version_v1)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(assign_contract_version_to_endpoint server_impl_1 contract_version_v1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint server_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(provision_server_implementation server_impl_1 interface_template_rest contract_version_v1)
(attach_operation_to_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(assign_contract_version_to_endpoint consumer_impl_1 contract_version_v1 operation_get_resource)
(unassign_operation_from_endpoint consumer_impl_1 operation_get_resource)
(provision_consumer_implementation consumer_impl_1 interface_template_grpc contract_version_v1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning scenario in PDDL for service API contract design. The goal is to provision server_impl_1, consumer_impl_1, api_contract_document_alpha, and api_contract_document_beta for runtime.


## Examining planning scenario  
- The task involves designing a service API contract using PDDL, focusing on planning.  
- The goal is to provision specific components: server_impl_1, consumer_impl_1, and two API contract documents, alpha and beta, for runtime.

## Examining planning scenario  
- All components (server_impl_1, consumer_impl_1, api_contract_document_alpha, api_contract_document_beta) are types of api_endpoint_specification, part of implementation_category or component_category.  
- They share actions like create_api_endpoint_specification and assign_interface_template_to_endpoint for provisioning.  

## Checking initialization state  
- Initially, none of the entities have entity_spec_created, meaning they need creation before provisioning.  
- Provisioning requires going through steps like entity_attached_to_contract_document and entity_assigned_contract_version.  

## Exploring provisioning steps  
- Creating each entity first with create_api_endpoint_specification sets entity_spec_created, with preconditions not entity_spec_created and not runtime_provisioned.  
- Next, assigning an interface template (rest, grpc, or websocket) is needed, ensuring entity_bound_to_interface_template for later provisioning.  

## Analyzing prerequisites  
- Attaching entities to contract documents involves actions like mark_implementation_compliant_after_release, requiring release_artifact_staged and test_verified.  
- Contract documents need publish actions like finalize_and_publish_contract_document to reach entity_attached_to_contract_document.

## Exploring extension requirements  
- Attaching an extension module to contract documents requires entity_validated, but first needs validation steps like validate_and_activate_endpoint.  
- No initial has_extension_module, and review_passed relies on finalize_extension_review, which needs extension modules, suggesting extension attachment is necessary.  

## Checking review process  
- To get contract_document_review_in_progress, need contract_document_staged_for_review, governed by policies, authentication binding, and test suite mapping.  
- Two actions, apply_governance_policy_review and confirm, set review_in_progress, differing on consumer bindings inclusion.  

## Analyzing staging process  
- Staging for review requires entity_validated, test suite registered, and release artifact mapped, with actions like apply_test_results_and_stage_contract_document.  
- Initial state shows contract documents linked to release artifact package_1 and test suites, but staging needs release artifact staged and test-verified.  

## Investigating release artifact readiness  
- Staging release artifact requires server and consumer readiness, indicating coupling with implementation states.  
- Test verification of release artifact is needed, but further details on achieving this are pending.

## Investigating implementation readiness  
- Allocating message channels for servers requires validation, operation binding, and channel mapping, setting the channel as allocated and marking the server ready.  
- Attaching authentication and marking implementation ready needs prior allocation and authentication binding, ensuring server readiness without requiring operation binding post-allocation.  

## Analyzing data schema utilization  
- Assigning data schemas to implementations sets the channel as engaged and the server ready, requiring a validated entity and available data schema.  
- Binding schemas to channels sets the channel as allocated and marked ready, freeing the data schema and requiring operation binding and engaged channel status.  

## Exploring consumer readiness  
- Consumer readiness follows a similar path, with data schema assignment setting the deploy as engaged and marking the consumer ready, using available schemas.  
- Binding schemas to consumer channels allocates the deploy and marks it ready, freeing schemas and requiring operation binding and engaged deploy status.  

## Checking serialization needs  
- Operations shared between server and consumer require sequential binding and unbinding, such as attaching operations to servers, allocating channels, then switching to consumers.  
- This serialization ensures proper allocation and readiness, especially when using shared resources like data schemas and operations.  

## Reviewing assembly requirements  
- Assembling release artifacts requires both server and consumer to be ready, marked ready, and have allocated channels and deploys.  
- The process leverages the readiness and allocation achieved through schema binding and channel allocation steps.

## Managing Authentication and Extensions  
- Authentication can't be bound to multiple endpoints simultaneously; unbinding from one (e.g., alpha) is needed before binding to another (e.g., beta).  
- Similarly, extensions must be detached from one contract document (e.g., alpha) before attaching to another (e.g., beta) for staging and review.  

## Reviewing Contract Processes  
- After staging for review, bind authentication and governance policies to beta, enabling governance policy and extension reviews, leading to a published state.  
- Contract versions are assigned and provisioned sequentially due to shared availability, ensuring proper attachment and provisioning for endpoints like alpha and beta.  

## Ensuring Implementation Compliance  
- Marking implementations compliant (e.g., server, consumer) requires readiness, marked readiness, and a tested, staged release artifact, resulting in attachment to contracts.  
- Post-compliance, contract versions can be assigned and provisioned, serializing actions across endpoints to manage shared resources like contract versions.

## Assigning Contract Versions  
- Alpha contract version v1 is assigned after ensuring the operation is bound, linking alpha to v1 and marking v1 unavailable.  
- After unassigning the operation, provisioning alpha with websocket interface makes v1 and websocket available again.  

## Managing Interface Allocation  
- Beta is assigned the freed websocket interface, binding it and making websocket unavailable.  
- Operations are attached to beta for validation, then unassigned after validation is complete.  

## Staging and Governance for Beta  
- Test suite integration is attached to beta's release artifact, registering and mapping it, making it unavailable.  
- Governance policy and authentication, previously attached to alpha, are detached and rebound to beta for governance review, setting beta in progress.  

## Handling Extensions  
- Extension module metrics is detached from alpha, making it available, then attached to beta.  
- This process ensures beta can proceed with staging and review, managing shared resources effectively.

## Creating Endpoints and Interfaces
- Create API endpoint specifications for server implementation, consumer implementation, and both contract documents (alpha and beta).

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
